summary: Large decompilation pass on src/melee/ty/toy, described by the author as “More vacuuming work.” The PR replaced many /// stubs in toy.c with C implementations, added numerous local toy/trophy/camera/light/display structs and externs needed to express data access, and adjusted toy.h declarations. The match report recorded +2680 matched bytes, 10 new full matches in main/melee/ty/toy, and 18 improved unmatched items, including several 90–99% toy functions and .sdata/.sdata2 progress. Review focused on keeping generated decomp output maintainable: remove useless sandbox comments, keep type definitions at the top of the file, prefer structured loops and continue over goto next_iter, and consider inline helpers for label-shaped boolean checks unless doing so hurts matching.
what_changed: Added implementations for many previously stubbed toy.c functions, including full matches reported for un_803064B8, un_8030663C, un_80307018, un_80308DC8, un_80311680, fn_80307E84, un_80306A48, un_803083D8, un_80311F5C, and un_80306954.

Improved many still-unmatched toy functions to high percentages, with examples from the report including un_80308F04 at 99.88%, un_803084A0 at 99.85%, un_80307BA0 at 99.95%, un_80305918 at about 98.7%, un_803062EC implemented, un_803063D4 at 90.3%, un_80306D70 at 82.6%, and .sdata/.sdata2 progress.

Added many local struct definitions in toy.c for matching data layout: ToyNameData, TyLightEntry, TyLightSymbol, tyUnkStruct, TyCameraData_, TyLightArray_, ToyData, tyLightData, tyDispData, Toy26B8, ToyJObjNode, ToyGlobals* structs, TyUnk25, PosArray, and PosArrayFull.

Added extern declarations for toy globals and data symbols such as un_804D6E50, un_804D6E68, un_804D6EBC, un_804D6E64, un_804D6E9C, un_804D6EC4, un_803FDD18, un_803FE3B8, un_803FE3DC, un_803B8844, un_803B8864, and un_803B889C.
Added includes needed by the new code: baselib/gobjgxlink.h, baselib/gobjobject.h, baselib/gobjplink.h, baselib/sobjlib.h, lb/lbvector.h, and sc/types.h.
Implemented trophy/language-related logic around trophy unlock bits and localized trophy name/index conversion, using lbLang_IsSettingJP/US and lbLang_IsSavedLanguageJP/US patterns.

Implemented or improved toy display/render/light/archive setup code involving HSD_GObj, HSD_CObj, HSD_LObj, HSD_JObj animation, HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress, lbArchive_80016DBC, DevText, fog, and erase color handling.
smart_moves: The author ran the permuter on 99% matches, then performed a self-review before requesting review, which is a useful workflow for large vacuuming/decomp PRs.
When review identified a do/while plus goto next_iter pattern in un_80305918, the author converted it to a for loop with continue while preserving the 98.71% match.

For a label-heavy inner loop in un_80305918, the author tried an inline bool helper as suggested but documented that it reduced the match from about 98.7% to about 96%; they retained labels and added a comment explaining the logical skip-list check.
Moved/kept type definitions at the top of toy.c in response to review, avoiding mid-file typedefs emitted by sandboxed/generated decompilation.
Added a checker to strip useless generated comments from sandboxed instances after review objected to comments like “Decompilation of ... / Unit: ...”.
Used explicit offset-based temporary structs to make archive, light, camera, JObj, LObj, text, and trophy data access understandable without overclaiming full semantic names.
decomp_lessons: For large vacuuming PRs, generated output should be cleaned before review: remove mechanical “Decompilation of” comments and avoid leaving type declarations in the middle of function regions.

A goto pattern can sometimes be rewritten into normal C control flow without hurting match; specifically, goto next_iter in a loop should often become continue, and do/while may become for when induction variables are obvious.

Inline helpers can clarify repeated label-shaped boolean checks, but do not force them if they materially damage the match. In this PR, converting the skip-list label pattern in un_80305918 to an inline dropped the match from about 98.7% to about 96%, so labels plus a clarifying comment were accepted.

When matching code uses unknown data layouts, local partial structs with offset fields and padding are acceptable stepping stones, especially for HSD archive/light/camera/object data, but keep them organized at the top of the file.

Locale-dependent trophy/toy code often branches on both current setting and saved language; preserving the exact lbLang_IsSettingJP/US and lbLang_IsSavedLanguageJP/US structure may matter for matching.
Review can tolerate labels in near-matches when they encode original assembly control flow, but reviewers prefer the most structured form that preserves the match.
naming_conventions: Used address-based names for unknown functions and globals, e.g. un_80305918, un_803064B8, un_804D6EBC, un_803FDD18, consistent with unresolved decomp symbols.

Used domain-prefixed provisional struct names for local toy system layouts, e.g. ToyNameData, ToyData, ToyDataJObj, ToyGlobalsS_, ToyGlobals2S_, TyLightEntry, TyLightSymbol, TyCameraData_, TyLightArray_, and tyDispData.
Used offset-style field names such as x0, x4, x8, xFC_idx, xCC_name, x144, x14C, x154, and pad ranges to document known offsets without inventing semantics.
Kept HSD type names for known engine objects: HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj, HSD_CObj, HSD_LObj, HSD_Archive, HSD_Text, HSD_FogDesc.
Used comments such as “/* 98.7% match */” and “/* Check if var_r25 is in skip list */” to document match status and intentional non-structured control flow.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Permuter was used on 99% matches before review.
Retained label-based control flow in un_80305918 where an inline helper changed code generation and reduced match quality from about 98.7% to about 96%.
Converted a do/while with goto next_iter into a for loop with continue while keeping the un_80305918 match at 98.71%.
Used partial structs and pointer arithmetic for exact offsets, such as ToyNameData fields x0/x2/x4/x6/x8/xA and TyLightEntry/TyLightSymbol offsets xFC_idx and xCC_name.
Preserved exact branch structure for language checks and save-language checks where it affected matching.
Accepted high-percent partial matches with explicit comments when full struct/control-flow recovery was not yet complete.
review_feedback: ribbanya asked to stop emitting generated comments like “Decompilation of un_803062EC / Unit: main/melee/ty/toy.”
ribbanya suggested teaching the tooling to create an inline function when it wants to create a label, noting that one label pattern was logically an inline bool whose result was checked.
ribbanya pointed out that the un_80305918 loop was a for loop and that goto next_iter should be continue.
ribbanya requested that type definitions be placed at the top of the file, possibly enforced with a static checker and then moved manually.
malvarezcastillo responded that useless generated comments were addressed with an added checker for sandboxed instances.
malvarezcastillo fixed the next_iter control-flow issue by converting the do/while plus goto pattern to a for loop with continue, preserving the 98.71% match.
malvarezcastillo partially addressed the inline-helper suggestion, reporting that an inline refactor dropped the match to about 96%, so they documented the logical pattern instead.
ribbanya accepted keeping labels in that case, saying it was fine as labels though a manual refactor would probably involve inlines.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or move the provisional toy structs from toy.c into shared headers.
Look for follow-up work that finishes the near matches in toy.c, especially un_80308F04, un_803084A0, un_80310324, un_80310660, un_80307BA0, un_8030715C, un_803078E4, and un_80305918.
Search for commits after PR 2147 that remove remaining label-heavy control flow in un_80305918 or introduce an inline skip-list helper without losing match.
Check whether the sandbox/static checker for removing generated comments and flagging mid-file typedefs was committed elsewhere or remained local workflow.
Investigate toy.h changes in this PR for any declaration cleanup or forward-declaration lessons not visible in the excerpt.
